perturbation.original_text: "Confidential Information" means any proprietary or confidential information that is competitively sensitive material or otherwise of value to a Party or its Affiliates and not generally known to the public, including trade secrets, scientific or technical information, design, invention, process, procedure, formula, improvements, product planning information, marketing strategies, financial information, information regarding operations, consumer and/or customer relationships, consumer and/or customer identities and profiles, sales estimates, business plans, and internal performance results relating to the past, present or future business activities of a Party or its Affiliates and the consumers, customers, clients and suppliers of any of the foregoing. Confidential Information includes such information as may be contained in or embodied by documents, substances, engineering and laboratory notebooks, reports, data, specifications, computer source code and object code, flow charts, databases, drawings, pilot plants or demonstration or operating facilities, diagrams, specifications, bills of material, equipment, prototypes and models, and any other tangible manifestation (including data in computer or other digital format) of the foregoing; provided, however, that Confidential Information does not include information that a receiving Party can show (a) has been published or has otherwise become available to the general public as part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement, (b) has been furnished or made known to the receiving Party without any obligation to keep it confidential by a third party under circumstances which are not known to the receiving Party to involve a breach of the third party's obligations to a Party or (c) was developed independently of information furnished or made available to the receiving Party as contemplated under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: "Confidential Information" means any information, regardless of its sensitivity or public availability, that either Party designates as confidential. This includes, but is not limited to, any data exchanged during the course of this agreement. Information is considered Confidential Information regardless of whether it: (a) has been published or has otherwise become available to the general public as part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement, (b) has been furnished or made known to the receiving Party without any obligation to keep it confidential by a third party under circumstances which are not known to the receiving Party to involve a breach of the third party's obligations to a Party or (c) was developed independently of information furnished or made available to the receiving Party as contemplated under this Agreement. However, any information designated as Confidential Information by the Shipper shall not be considered to be Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' excludes information generally known to the public. The modified definition includes 'any information' designated as confidential, 'regardless of its sensitivity or public availability', creating an in-text contradiction within the definition itself. The caveat at the end, stating that any information designated as Confidential Information by the Shipper shall not be considered to be Confidential Information contradicts the start of the clause, as it designates ALL information regardless of sensitivity or public availability as confidential. This creates uncertainty about what the standard is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 From and after the Effective Date, each Party shall hold, and shall cause its Affiliates and its and their respective directors, managers, officers, employees, agents, consultants, advisors, contractors, and other representatives (collectively, "Representatives") to hold all Confidential Information of the other Party in strict confidence, with at least the same degree of care that applies to such Party's confidential and proprietary information and shall not use such Confidential Information except in connection with its performance or acceptance of services hereunder and shall not release or disclose such Confidential Information to any other Person, except its Representatives. Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Section 7 by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of "Confidential Information" significantly broadens its scope to include "any information...regardless of its sensitivity or public availability," directly contradicting the original definition, which specifically excludes information generally known to the public. This creates a direct conflict with Section 7.1, which mandates strict confidentiality for the "Confidential Information of the other Party". The modified text creates a contradiction within itself by stating that the Shipper's confidential information is not confidential, and then it contradicts section 7.1 because all information, confidential or not, must be kept secret. This makes the obligation to maintain confidentiality in section 7.1 uncertain and unenforceable, as the scope of 'Confidential Information' is now ambiguous and internally inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Shipper guarantees that during each Contract Year, Shipper will meet its Quarterly Volume Commitment or, in the event it fails to do so, shall remit to MPL the Quarterly Deficiency Payment pursuant to Section 3.5. All volumes moved by Shipper on the Pipeline will be subject to the applicable Tariff Rates and proration policies, as may be amended from time to time in accordance with FERC methodologies and as provided herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Shipper guarantees that during each Contract Year, Shipper will meet its Quarterly Volume Commitment unless the Shipper is unable to meet the volume commitment, in which event, the Shipper is exempt from the terms and conditions in this agreement. All volumes moved by Shipper on the Pipeline will be subject to the applicable Tariff Rates and proration policies, as may be amended from time to time in accordance with FERC methodologies and as provided herein.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Shipper *will* meet its Quarterly Volume Commitment, or pay a deficiency. The modified version adds a clause that exempts the Shipper from the terms and conditions in this agreement if unable to meet the volume commitment, negating the entire purpose of the volume commitment agreement and potentially nullifying Section 3.5 (Quarterly Deficiency Payment) of the agreement, by creating a loophole.
perturbation.location: 3. Volume Commitment and Quarterly Deficiency Charges
perturbation.contradicted_location: 3.5 Subject to the provisions of Section 5 of this Agreement, if the volume of each Product shipped by Shipper on the Pipeline during the Quarter is less than the applicable Quarterly Volume Commitment for that Product then, in addition to paying any amounts incurred by Shipper pursuant to Section 3.4 with respect to Shipper Deliveries for such Quarter, Shipper shall also pay MPL a deficiency payment (the "Quarterly Deficiency Payment"); equal to the product of: (a) the difference between the applicable Quarterly Volume Commitment for that Product for such Quarter and the volume of Shipper Deliveries of that Product on the Pipeline for such Quarter (the "Deficiency Volume"); and (b) the applicable Tariff Rate for that Product for such Quarter. Each Product to which the Shipper is obligated to move as part of its committed volumes will be added together for one total Quarterly Deficiency Payment. Shipper's transportation of commodities outside this Agreement's definition of Product will not satisfy the obligation to move committed Product volume. All Product barrels moved by Shipper in excess of its Quarterly Volume Commitment will be attributed to Shipper's movements on the Pipeline as a Regular Shipper, subject to the applicable Rules & Regulations of the Tariff.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.5 Subject to the provisions of Section 5 of this Agreement, if the volume of each Product shipped by Shipper on the Pipeline during the Quarter is less than the applicable Quarterly Volume Commitment for that Product then, in addition to paying any amounts incurred by Shipper pursuant to Section 3.4 with respect to Shipper Deliveries for such Quarter, Shipper shall also pay MPL a deficiency payment (the "Quarterly Deficiency Payment"); equal to the product of: (a) the difference between the applicable Quarterly Volume Commitment for that Product for such Quarter and the volume of Shipper Deliveries of that Product on the Pipeline for such Quarter (the "Deficiency Volume"); and (b) the applicable Tariff Rate for that Product for such Quarter. Each Product to which the Shipper is obligated to move as part of its committed volumes will be added together for one total Quarterly Deficiency Payment. Shipper's transportation of commodities outside this Agreement's definition of Product will not satisfy the obligation to move committed Product volume. All Product barrels moved by Shipper in excess of its Quarterly Volume Commitment will be attributed to Shipper's movements on the Pipeline as a Regular Shipper, subject to the applicable Rules & Regulations of the Tariff.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant contradiction. The original text mandates that the Shipper meets its Quarterly Volume Commitment or pays a deficiency. The changed text provides an exemption if the Shipper is unable to meet the commitment, which directly conflicts with Section 3.5's obligation to pay a 'Quarterly Deficiency Payment' for failing to meet the commitment. This creates a loophole that undermines the enforceability of the volume commitment and contradicts the intent of Section 3.5.

perturbation.original_text: 10.4 Upon termination of this Agreement for reasons other than a default by Shipper, pursuant to any provisions of this Agreement or any other termination of this Agreement initiated by Shipper pursuant to Section 5, Shipper shall have the right to require MPL to enter into a new transportation service agreement with Shipper that (a) is consistent with the terms and objectives set forth in this Agreement and (b) has commercial terms that are, in the aggregate, equal to or more favorable to Shipper than fair market value terms as would be agreed by similarly-situated parties negotiating at arm's length provided.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.4 Upon termination of this Agreement for reasons other than a default by Shipper, pursuant to any provisions of this Agreement or any other termination of this Agreement initiated by Shipper pursuant to Section 5, Shipper shall *not* have the right to require MPL to enter into a new transportation service agreement with Shipper that (a) is consistent with the terms and objectives set forth in this Agreement and (b) has commercial terms that are, in the aggregate, equal to or more favorable to Shipper than fair market value terms as would be agreed by similarly-situated parties negotiating at arm's length provided.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause gives Shipper the right to demand a new, favorable transportation agreement in certain termination scenarios. The modified clause explicitly denies the Shipper that right. This direct reversal of a right creates a clear in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_location: 10.4 Upon termination of this Agreement for reasons other than a default by Shipper, pursuant to any provisions of this Agreement or any other termination of this Agreement initiated by Shipper pursuant to Section 5, Shipper shall *not* have the right to require MPL to enter into a new transportation service agreement with Shipper that (a) is consistent with the terms and objectives set forth in this Agreement and (b) has commercial terms that are, in the aggregate, equal to or more favorable to Shipper than fair market value terms as would be agreed by similarly-situated parties negotiating at arm's length provided.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.4 Upon termination of this Agreement for reasons other than a default by Shipper, pursuant to any provisions of this Agreement or any other termination of this Agreement initiated by Shipper pursuant to Section 5, Shipper shall *not* have the right to require MPL to enter into a new transportation service agreement with Shipper that (a) is consistent with the terms and objectives set forth in this Agreement and (b) has commercial terms that are, in the aggregate, equal to or more favorable to Shipper than fair market value terms as would be agreed by similarly-situated parties negotiating at arm's length provided.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants Shipper the right to demand a new transportation agreement in specific termination scenarios. The changed text explicitly denies this right. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict with the contradicted text, which is the changed text itself. This constitutes a structural flaw, as the clause now has conflicting implications within the document, creating uncertainty about the Shipper's rights upon termination. Since the change creates a direct conflict by negating a previously granted right, the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' is present.
